FreshnessDTO.description: When this was true and when it was computed (doc 10: freshness is mandatory).

as_of.description: Knowledge cutoff: nothing learned after this was used.
as_of.title: As Of

diagnostics.description: Typed numeric facts about how the value was reached, e.g. `anchor_offset_days: -3` meaning the anchor bar sat 3 days before the target.
diagnostics.title: Diagnostics

quality_flags.description: Opaque tags describing data conditions, e.g. `reference-version-drift`. Test membership; do not parse.
quality_flags.title: Quality Flags

FreshnessDTO.title: FreshnessDTO

InstrumentDTO.description: The instrument the metric describes, in canonical terms only.

id.description: Nivesh's internal instrument identifier — never a vendor symbol.
id.title: Id

InstrumentDTO.title: InstrumentDTO

LineageDTO.description:
The "why?" panel's raw material (doc 10: lineage is a first-class capability).

`contributing` names the observations that actually determined the value —
typically two — while `scanned_count` reports how many the feature supplied. That
split keeps the response proportional to the answer instead of to the history
behind it, without hiding how much data was considered.

parameters.description: The arguments the feature was invoked with.
parameters.title: Parameters

reference_version.description: The pinned reference-data snapshot in force at compute time.
reference_version.title: Reference Version

scanned_count.description: How many observations the feature supplied, contributing or not.
scanned_count.title: Scanned Count

source_refs.description: Opaque handles for every raw payload this value derives from.
source_refs.title: Source Refs

LineageDTO.title: LineageDTO

MetricDTO.description: The number, or an explicit account of why there isn't one.

formula_version.description: The methodology version that produced this value. A formula change is a new version, never a silent edit.
formula_version.title: Formula Version

unavailable_reason.description: Why no value could be produced. Present exactly when status is UNAVAILABLE.
unavailable_reason.title: Unavailable Reason

unit.description: `ratio` — unitless. 0.12 means +12%, not a currency amount.
unit.title: Unit

value.description: The value, or null when status is UNAVAILABLE. Never 0 as a stand-in for a missing input.
value.title: Value

MetricDTO.title: MetricDTO

ObservationRefDTO.description: One canonical observation that contributed to the value.

source_ref.description: Opaque, stable handle for the immutable raw payload behind this observation. Not a storage path; resolve it via the lineage API.
source_ref.title: Source Ref

ObservationRefDTO.title: ObservationRefDTO

OneYearReturnResponse.description: The endpoint's response — one metric, fully traced.
OneYearReturnResponse.title: OneYearReturnResponse

ResultStatus.description: Whether an analytic produced a value or declined to.
ResultStatus.title: ResultStatus

info.description: Read-only market analytics. Every derived value is served with its lineage and freshness, so any number can be traced to the raw records and the formula version that produced it.
info.title: Nivesh Terminal API

200.description: The metric with its lineage and freshness. A 200 with `status: UNAVAILABLE` means the instrument is known but the metric could not be computed; `unavailable_reason` says why.

404.description: No instrument with this identifier exists.

get.summary: One-year total return for an instrument
